steps.title: Substitute x = 4 into the function
steps.content:
We need to find $f(4)$. Since $f(x) = \frac{1}{2}(x+6)$, substitute $x = 4$:

$$f(4) = \frac{1}{2}(4 + 6)$$

steps.title: Simplify and evaluate
steps.content: Inside the parentheses: $4 + 6 = 10$. $$f(4) = \frac{1}{2}(10) = 5$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
